title: Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 77) to amend chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, to provide for en bloc consideration in resolutions of disapproval for "midnight rules", and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This House resolution makes it easier for Congress to quickly reject rules made by a president who is leaving office. These are called midnight rules because presidents often make them in their last days in power. Instead of voting on each rule one by one, Congress can vote against many of them together at the same time. The resolution speeds things up by skipping normal procedures and only allowing one hour of discussion.
last_action_text: Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

sections.title: House Resolution 122
sections.text: This resolution was passed by the House of Representatives on February eleventh, two thousand twenty five. The resolution says that the House can now consider a bill called House Resolution seventy seven. This bill would change the law to allow Congress to vote against multiple midnight rules at the same time. Midnight rules are regulations that a president creates right before leaving office. All usual procedural objections against discussing this bill are set aside. The bill will be treated as if it has already been read out loud. All usual procedural objections against parts of the bill are also set aside. The House will move straight to a final vote on the bill without other motions in between, except for two things. First, there will be one hour of debate time split equally between the chair and top minority member of the Judiciary Committee or people they choose to speak for them. Second, there will be one chance for a motion to send the bill back to committee for changes.
